nodes.title: MATH2130 Numerical Methods
nodes.content: Course manual for Numerical Methods at the University of Guelph, covering computational techniques to solve mathematical problems that are difficult or impossible to solve by hand.

nodes.title: Mathematical Background
nodes.content: Foundational concepts including error analysis, calculus theorems, Taylor series, and floating-point math essential for numerical analysis.

nodes.title: Absolute Error
nodes.content: The magnitude of the difference between the true value p and the approximation p: |p - p*|.

nodes.title: Relative Error
nodes.content: The error scaled by the true value: |p - p*| / |p|. Useful when comparing quantities of different scales.

nodes.title: Intermediate Value Theorem (IVT)
nodes.content: If f(x) is continuous on [a, b], then for any k between f(a) and f(b), there exists a c in (a, b) such that f(c) = k.

nodes.title: Mean Value Theorem (MVT)
nodes.content: If f(x) is continuous on [a, b] and differentiable on (a, b), there exists c in (a, b) such that f'(c) = (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a).

nodes.title: Taylor's Theorem
nodes.content: Approximates a function near a point x0 using a polynomial and an error (remainder) term. Central to deriving numerical methods.

nodes.title: Big-O Notation
nodes.content: Describes the asymptotic behavior of a function or error as a parameter (like stepsize h or n) approaches a limit.

nodes.title: Floating Point Math
nodes.content: How computers represent and calculate real numbers using finite precision, leading to rounding and chopping errors.

nodes.title: Root-Finding Methods
nodes.content: Iterative algorithms used to find solutions to equations of the form f(x) = 0.

nodes.title: Bisection Method
nodes.content: A root-finding method that repeatedly halves an interval [a, b] where f(a) and f(b) have opposite signs. Guaranteed to converge.

nodes.title: Method of False Position
nodes.content: Similar to bisection but uses a linear interpolant between f(a) and f(b) to find the next estimate.

nodes.title: Secant Method
nodes.content: An iterative root-finding method that uses the secant line through two points. Does not require bracketing the root.

nodes.title: Newton's Method (Roots)
nodes.content: Uses the formula x_{n+1} = xn - f(xn)/f'(xn). Converges quadratically if the initial guess is close enough to the root.

nodes.title: Optimization
nodes.content: Methods for finding the maximum or minimum of an objective function.

nodes.title: Newton's Method for Optimization
nodes.content: Applies Newton's root-finding method to the derivative f'(x) to find critical points where f'(x) = 0.

nodes.title: Gradient Descent
nodes.content: An iterative optimization algorithm that moves in the direction of the negative gradient to find a local minimum.

nodes.title: Curve-Fitting & Interpolation
nodes.content: Creating a polynomial that passes through a discrete set of data points.

nodes.title: Newton Polynomial
nodes.content: A form of the interpolating polynomial expressed using divided differences. Efficient for adding new data points.

nodes.title: Divided Differences
nodes.content: Recursive formula used to calculate the coefficients of a Newton interpolating polynomial.

nodes.title: Numerical Integration
nodes.content: Approximating the definite integral of a function using discrete samples.

nodes.title: Riemann Sums
nodes.content: Basic approximation of area under a curve using rectangles. Errors converge slowly.

nodes.title: Trapezoid Rule
nodes.content: Approximates the area under a curve using trapezoids (piecewise linear interpolation).

nodes.title: Simpson's Rule
nodes.content: Approximates area using parabolas (piecewise quadratic interpolation). Generally more accurate than the Trapezoid rule.

nodes.title: Initial Value Problems (IVPs)
nodes.content: Solving differential equations numerically starting from a known initial condition.

nodes.title: Euler's Method
nodes.content: The simplest IVP solver; uses the slope at the current point to step forward: y_{i+1} = yi + h*f(ti, yi).

nodes.title: Runge-Kutta Methods
nodes.content: Family of iterative methods (including Midpoint Method and RK4) that use weighted averages of slopes for higher accuracy.

nodes.title: Butcher Tableaus
nodes.content: A tabular format used to represent the coefficients of Runge-Kutta methods.

nodes.title: Finite Difference Differentiation
nodes.content: Numerical approximations of derivatives using discrete function values and a stepsize h.
